name: Andaman and Nicobar Islands Labour Laws
overview: Union Territory-specific labour-law requirements applicable to establishments operating in the Andaman and Nicobar Islands. Local Shops and Establishments, working-hours, leave, registration, notice and administrative requirements continue as applicable and are not superseded merely because the four central Labour Codes commenced on 21 November 2025. Minimum-wage, wage-payment, occupational-safety, contract-labour, inter-State migrant-worker and social-security obligations must be assessed together with the applicable Labour Code, the identity of the appropriate government, saved legacy notifications and any rules or notifications issued for the Union Territory.

authority.name: Labour Department, Andaman and Nicobar Administration

rules.title: Review Shops and Establishments Registration
rules.description: Determine whether registration under the applicable Shops and Establishments legislation is required. Treat this local registration obligation as distinct from registration or licensing that may separately arise under a central Labour Code.

citation.reference: Applicable Andaman and Nicobar Islands Shops and Establishments legislation

rules.title: Maintain Statutory Registers and Employment Records
rules.description: Maintain statutory registers, employee records and prescribed notices under applicable Union Territory labour legislation. Maintain Labour Code records separately where a central Code, saved rule, notification or scheme also applies.

citation.reference: Applicable Andaman and Nicobar Islands Labour Rules

rules.title: Display Statutory Notices
rules.description: Display statutory notices and employee information required under applicable Union Territory labour legislation, together with any notices or abstracts separately required under an applicable Labour Code, saved rule or notification.

citation.reference: Applicable Andaman and Nicobar Islands Labour Rules

rules.title: Review Working Hours, Overtime, Leave and Weekly Holidays
rules.description: Comply with working-hours, overtime, leave and weekly-holiday provisions applicable under the Union Territory's Shops and Establishments framework. Separately assess the Occupational Safety, Health and Working Conditions Code, 2020 for establishments and worker categories falling within that Code.

citation.reference: Applicable Andaman and Nicobar Islands Shops and Establishments Rules
Occupational Safety, Health and Working Conditions Code, 2020

rules.title: Apply Current Minimum Wage Notifications
rules.description: Pay employees at least the minimum wage applicable to the relevant employment, skill category, location and wage period. From 21 November 2025, assess minimum-wage obligations under the Code on Wages, 2019 together with the latest notification issued by the appropriate government. Continue using a legacy minimum-wage notification only to the extent it is preserved by section 69 of the Code and has not been replaced or repealed.

citation.reference: Andaman and Nicobar Administration Minimum Wage Notifications

citation.section: Minimum-wage, wage-period, overtime and record provisions

rules.title: Monitor Labour Department Notifications
rules.description: Review notifications, circulars, wage revisions, rules, exemptions and compliance updates issued by the Andaman and Nicobar Administration. Confirm whether each instrument was issued under local legislation, a saved repealed enactment or one of the four Labour Codes.

citation.reference: Andaman and Nicobar Administration Labour Notifications

rules.title: Determine the Appropriate Government Under the Labour Codes
rules.description: For each establishment and obligation, determine whether the Central Government or the competent Union Territory authority is the appropriate government. Apply the corresponding rules, notifications, wage rates, registrations, licences, forms and enforcement authorities. Do not assume that the Central Rules automatically replace every Union Territory instrument or that local Shops and Establishments requirements have been repealed.

citation.reference: Implementation of the four Labour Codes from 21 November 2025
Appropriate-government and rule-making provisions under the four Labour Codes

exceptions.title: Local Laws Continue Independently
exceptions.description: The commencement of the four Labour Codes does not by itself repeal Union Territory Shops and Establishments, holiday, leave or other local labour requirements that are outside the central enactments expressly repealed by the Codes.

exceptions.title: Saved Minimum Wage Notifications
exceptions.description: A minimum-wage notification issued under a repealed enactment may continue only to the extent preserved by section 69 of the Code on Wages, 2019 and until it is replaced or repealed under the Code.

exceptions.title: Central Rules Are Jurisdiction Dependent
exceptions.description: The Code on Wages (Central) Rules, 2026 and other Central Labour Code Rules apply where the Central Government is the appropriate government. Other establishments must use the rules and notifications issued by their competent government.

recommendations.title: Annual Labour Compliance Audit
recommendations.description: Conduct an annual audit covering local registrations, payroll compliance, statutory records, Labour Code applicability and inspection readiness.

recommendations.title: Digital Compliance Calendar
recommendations.description: Maintain a digital calendar for statutory filings, inspections, licence renewals, wage revisions and compliance due dates.

recommendations.title: Maintain a Wage Notification Register
recommendations.description: Record the issuing authority, employment category, skill level, location, effective date, variable dearness allowance and replacement status of every minimum-wage notification applied to employees.

recommendations.title: Review Labour Code Instruments Monthly
recommendations.description: Check Ministry and Andaman and Nicobar Administration publications monthly for new rules, notifications, wage revisions, forms, authorities, exemptions and transition directions.

sources.name: Labour Department, Andaman and Nicobar Administration
sources.authority: Labour Department, Andaman and Nicobar Administration

sources.name: Ministry of Labour & Employment Labour Codes Portal
sources.authority: Ministry of Labour & Employment, Government of India

sources.name: India Code
sources.authority: Legislative Department, Ministry of Law and Justice, Government of India

sources.name: Code on Wages (Central) Rules, 2026
sources.authority: Ministry of Labour & Employment, Government of India

governance.changeSummary: Updated the Andaman and Nicobar Islands record for the Labour Code transition without superseding local Union Territory laws. Added Code on Wages repeal-and-savings treatment, appropriate-government determination, separation of local Shops and Establishments obligations from central Code obligations, and monthly monitoring of Union Territory implementation instruments.
